text.title: Kubernetes Ingress Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for Kubernetes Ingress deployments. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Production Ingress controller Deployments MUST have `replicas: ≥ 3`. Single-replica controllers are forbidden in production.

rationale.source: Kubernetes Ingress production checklist + Ingress-Nginx docs

rule.rationale: A single Ingress pod failing or being evicted causes a full HTTP outage for every route. Three replicas survive one pod loss + one rolling-restart at a time.

rule.statement: Every Ingress controller Deployment MUST have a PodDisruptionBudget with `minAvailable: 2` (for `replicas: 3`). The PDB MUST select the same labels as the Deployment.
rule.rationale: Without a PDB, voluntary disruptions (node drain, cluster upgrade) can evict all controller pods at once. The PDB blocks the drain until at least 2 pods remain ready.

rule.statement: Ingress controller Deployments MUST set `podAntiAffinity` with `topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname` to prevent two controller pods landing on the same node. `requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution` is the production default.
rule.rationale: Without anti-affinity, all controller pods may co-locate on one node; that node failing takes the whole Ingress tier down.

rule.statement: Multi-AZ clusters MUST set `topologySpreadConstraints` with `topologyKey: topology.kubernetes.io/zone` and `whenUnsatisfiable: ScheduleAnyway` (or `DoNotSchedule` if enough nodes exist). Single-AZ controller pools in a multi-AZ cluster are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without zone spread, all controller pods may end up in one AZ; an AZ failure removes the Ingress tier entirely.

rule.statement: Per-Ingress annotations MUST use the namespace of the chosen controller: `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/*` for ingress-nginx, `haproxy.org/*` for HAProxy Ingress, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/*` for Traefik. Mixing namespaces is forbidden — annotations from a different controller are silently ignored.
rule.rationale: Cross-controller annotations are a top LLM bug: rate-limit / CORS / body-size silently never apply because the annotation belongs to a controller that is not installed.
